methods.description: This method describes the process for preparing sediment trap samples, extracting and derivatizing lipids, and analyzing them using Gas Chromatography-Mass Spectrometry (GC-MS) for identification and quantification. The protocol is based on methods described by Kiriakoulakis et al. (2001) and Wolff et al. (2011).

steps.description: Lipid analyses were performed on 1 / 8 wet aliquots resulting from the splitting of original samples. Because of the low amount of material collected in some cups, 1 / 8 wet aliquots were combined prior to the lipid analyses (Supplement). Some samples were lost upon recovery of sediment traps and two were contaminated with fish debris and therefore not included in lipid analyses. Full details of all sediment trap samples and those included in lipid analyses is provided in the Supplement (Tables S1-S5).
steps.searchable_summary: Split original sediment trap samples into 1/8 wet aliquots and combine aliquots with low material amount. Document sample exclusions.

steps.description: Briefly, separate 1 / 8 aliquots were spiked with an internal standard (5α(H)-cholestane)...
steps.searchable_summary: Spike sample aliquots with 5α(H)-cholestane internal standard.
Extract lipids from samples (on filters) by sonication using a dichloromethane:methanol (9:1) mixture.
Perform transmethylation of the lipid extract using methanolic acetyl chloride.

steps.description: ...and silylated (bis(trimethylsilyl)trifluoroacetamide; 1 % trimethylsilane chloride; 30-50 µL; 40 • C; 0.5-1 h).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform silylation of transmethylated lipids using bis(trimethylsilyl)trifluoroacetamide and trimethylsilane chloride.

steps.description: Gas chromatography -mass spectrometry (GC-MS) analyses were carried out using a GC Trace 1300 fitted with a split-splitless injector, using helium as a carrier gas (2 mL min −1 ) and column DB-5MS (60 m × 0.25 mm (i.d.), film thickness 0.1 µm, non-polar solution of 5 % phenyl and 95 % methyl silicone).
steps.searchable_summary: Configure GC-MS system (GC Trace 1300) with split-splitless injector, helium carrier gas, and DB-5MS column.

steps.description: The GC oven was programmed after 1 min from 60 to 170 • C at 6 • C min −1 , then from 170 to 315 • C at 2.5 • C min −1 and held at 315 • C for 15 min.
steps.searchable_summary: Program the GC oven temperature profile for chromatographic separation.
outputs: GC oven programmed with temperature profile

steps.description: The eluent from the GC was transferred directly to the electron impact source of a Thermoquest ISQMS single quadrupole mass spectrometer. Typical operating conditions were as follows: ionization potential, 70 eV; source temperature, 215 • C; trap current, 300 µA.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure MS detector (Thermoquest ISQMS) with electron impact source and specified operating conditions.

steps.description: Mass data were collected at a resolution of 600, cycling every second from 50-600 Th...
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire mass spectral data from the MS detector with specified resolution and scan range.
Process raw GC-MS data using Xcalibur software.

steps.description: Compounds were identified either by comparison of their mass spectra and relative retention indices with those available in the literature and/or by comparison with authentic standards.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify compounds by comparing mass spectra and retention indices to literature data or authentic standards.
inputs: Processed GC-MS data (mass spectra, retention indices)

steps.description: Quantitative data were calculated by comparison of peak areas of the internal standard with those of the compounds of interest, using the total ion current (TIC) chromatogram.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate quantitative compound concentrations based on peak area ratios relative to the internal standard using the Total Ion Current (TIC) chromatogram.
inputs: Processed GC-MS data (peak areas from TIC)

steps.description: The relative response factors of the analytes were determined individually for 36 representative fatty acids, sterols and alkenones using authentic standards. Response factors for analytes where standards were unavailable were assumed to be identical to those of available compounds of the same class.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine relative response factors for representative analytes using authentic standards and apply class-based assumptions for others.
